items.title: Sustainability Reporting Season – 30 Jun 2026
items.desc: ASIC flagged six recurring compliance gaps in early mandatory climate reports. June 30, 2026 reporting season deadline applies to Group 2 entities. Ensure compliance with AASB S2 and Chapter 2M of the Corporations Act 2001.

items.title: ASIC 2026 Enforcement Priorities – Private Credit Focus
items.desc: ASIC added private credit practices, financial reporting misconduct, insurance claims handling and misleading pricing to 2026 enforcement priorities. Doubled investigations and nearly doubled court cases vs prior year.

items.title: Digital Assets Framework Act – Royal Assent 8 Apr 2026
items.desc: Corporations Amendment (Digital Assets Framework) Act 2026 received Royal Assent. ASIC roadmap: registration for digital asset/tokenised custody platforms from Apr 2027, full compliance by Oct 2027.

items.title: FFSP AFSL Exemptions Commence – 8 Apr 2027
items.desc: Parliament passed new AFSL exemptions for foreign financial services providers. Transitional relief extended to 31 Mar 2027. New regime commences 8 Apr 2027 with clearer eligibility thresholds and compliance obligations.

items.title: Whistleblower Programs Under Increased Scrutiny
items.desc: ASIC Report 827 found 30% of companies don't regularly review whistleblower program effectiveness. ASIC will directly contact companies with inadequate practices and increase monitoring through 2026.

items.title: CSLR Levy Notice Payment – 4 Jun 2026
items.desc: Compensation scheme of last resort (CSLR) levy notice now available. Payment due by Thursday 4 June 2026 to avoid penalties. All in-scope AFSL holders must pay.

items.title: AML/CTF Officer Notification – 30 May 2026
items.desc: Existing reporting entities enrolled before 31 Mar 2026 must notify AUSTRAC of their AML/CTF compliance officer by 30 May 2026 under the Transitional Rules 2026.

items.title: Tranche 2 Entities Obligations Commence – 1 Jul 2026
items.desc: AML/CTF obligations commence for lawyers, accountants, real estate agents and jewellers. Must enrol with AUSTRAC by 29 Jul 2026. Requires documented AML/CTF program, CDD and suspicious matter reporting.

items.title: Unified AML/CTF Program Required – 31 Mar 2026
items.desc: Part A/Part B distinction removed. Existing reporting entities must implement unified, outcomes-focused AML/CTF program linking risk assessments to controls. Travel rule obligations also now apply.

items.title: AUSTRAC 2026 Risk Snapshots Released – 12 May 2026
items.desc: AUSTRAC released updated ML/TF/PF risk snapshots. Key focus: AI-enabled financial crime, virtual assets, near-instant settlement risks. Use as input for risk assessment updates.

items.title: FATF Mutual Evaluation – 2026
items.desc: Australia's next Financial Action Task Force mutual evaluation is scheduled for 2026. Will assess implementation and effectiveness of AML/CTF measures, including new Tranche 2 reforms.

items.title: Annual Compliance Reporting Moves to Financial Year – FY2026-27
items.desc: Compliance reporting period changing to 1 Jul – 30 Jun. First report due for FY2026-27 period. Submit within 3 months (by 30 Sep 2027). AFSL holders providing designated services must lodge.

items.title: AER Separates from ACCC – 1 Jul 2026
items.desc: Competition and Consumer Amendment (Australian Energy Regulator Separation) Act 2025 takes effect. AER becomes standalone Commonwealth entity with own staffing and budgetary autonomy.

items.title: 2026-27 Network Charges Approved – May 2026
items.desc: AER published approved electricity distributors' annual pricing proposals for ACT, NSW, NT, QLD, SA, TAS for 2026-27. Network charges mostly increasing due to transmission costs and inflation.

items.title: Electricity Retail Code Obligations – 1 Jul 2026
items.desc: AER and ACCC joint compliance bulletin issued. Retailers must comply with new Electricity Retail Code obligations effective 1 July 2026. Enhanced reporting on concessions and rebates required.

items.title: Rate of Return Instrument Review – Final Dec 2026
items.desc: AER's 2026 RORI review underway. Eligible Experts' report released. Final decision expected December 2026. Will affect allowed returns for regulated electricity and gas networks.

items.title: Hardship Customer Better Offer Rule – Dec 2026
items.desc: From December 2026, hardship customers must pay no more than their deemed better offer where available. Compliance audits will test retailer controls for this requirement.

items.title: $15.9M Budget for NEM Reforms Implementation
items.desc: 2026-27 Federal Budget allocated $15.9 million over four years for AER to implement National Electricity Market review recommendations. Enhanced regulatory focus on wholesale market settings.
